name: add-macos-statusbar
tagline: Compiles and installs a native macOS menu-bar app showing NanoClaw's running status with Start/Stop/Restart controls.
verdict: Safe, well-structured installer: deterministic steps, upfront tool checks, idempotent re-run handling, no network egress, secrets, or destructive ops. Weak spots: description lacks explicit 'use when' trigger framing, no failure handling once compile/load/verify starts, referenced files (statusbar.swift, REMOVE.md) can't be verified to exist, and there's no.

highlights.summary: Description states what it does but never frames when to invoke it (no 'use when'/trigger phrasing).
Only pre-flight failures (platform, missing swiftc) are handled; no guidance if swiftc compile fails, launchctl load fails, or Phase 3 shows no PID.
References ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/add/src/statusbar.swift and REMOVE.md as skill-local files; no file inventory was available to confirm they exist.
